page: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It is equipped with 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81,307.29 and a PassMark CPU score of 55,934.8. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4,502.07 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 219.74 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The instance also demonstrates high throughput in Redis SET operations, processing 75.3 million operations per second, and static web serving at 45.13 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1,387.4 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression speed of 23.57 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3en.24xlarge offers high resource density, combining massive local NVMe storage capacity with high-speed networking and substantial memory. This makes it highly suitable for I/O-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and distributed file systems. However, the lack of hardware accelerators makes it poorly suited for heavy LLM text generation, where it exhibits poor performance, such as 5.49 tokens per second on medium models. Appropriate workloads include high-throughput database operations, data warehousing, and large-scale static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor, powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. It features 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, with Redis SET operations reaching 75.3 million operations per second and static web serving at 45.13 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core CPU performance is weak, and LLM text generation speeds are poor due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. This instance offers high resource density for storage-heavy deployments, making it highly suitable for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput static web serving, while being less optimal for compute-bound single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 60 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-I/O workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory instance featuring sixty terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity and hundred gigabit baseline networking.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors at 3.1 GHz
60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-I/O applications
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput Redis operations
Best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and static web serving
